disclaimer: demo data — fictionalised projects, invented prices/figures for POC only

projects.name: Marjan Bay Residences
projects.location: Al Marjan Island, Ras Al Khaimah
projects.developerTrackRecord: 4 delivered projects, avg. 2 months early handover
projects.paymentPlan: 10% down / 50% during construction / 40% on handover; post-handover option to 2029
projects.roiNotes: Al Marjan short-term rental yields ~9–11% gross (demo figure); Wynn Al Marjan casino-resort opening 2027 is the area's main demand driver
comparisons: vs JVC: higher gross yield (~9–11% vs ~6–7%) but longer commute to central Dubai and a newer, less-proven resale market
vs Al Zorah: similar beachfront premium, but Marjan has the Wynn catalyst

faqs.q: Is there a post-handover payment plan?
faqs.a: Yes — 40% on handover can be spread to 2029 at 0% interest on selected units.
Thinner than Dubai today, but transaction volume on Al Marjan has roughly tripled since the Wynn announcement (demo figure). We position it as a 3–5 year hold.

projects.name: Pulse Tower JVC
projects.developerTrackRecord: 4 delivered projects, avg. 2 months early handover
projects.roiNotes: JVC gross yields ~6–7%; one of Dubai's most rented communities, very liquid resale
comparisons: vs Marjan Bay: lower yield but inside Dubai, faster resale, proven tenant demand
vs Dubai Science Park: comparable price point, JVC has stronger short-let demand

faqs.q: Can I short-let it on Airbnb?
faqs.a: Yes, JVC permits holiday-home licensing; many of our owners run it as a short-let.

projects.name: Meydan Vista
projects.location: Meydan, Mohammed Bin Rashid City, Dubai
projects.developerTrackRecord: 4 delivered projects, avg. 2 months early handover
projects.roiNotes: Meydan capital appreciation play; rental yields ~5–6% but strong price growth near Downtown
comparisons: vs JVC: higher entry, lower yield, but stronger capital appreciation and prestige address
vs DLRC: more central, faster access to Downtown/DIFC

faqs.q: Is this better for rental income or appreciation?
faqs.a: Appreciation. For pure yield we'd point you at JVC or Marjan Bay; Meydan is a capital-growth hold.

projects.name: Al Zorah Cove
projects.developerTrackRecord: 4 delivered projects, avg. 2 months early handover
projects.roiNotes: Emerging waterfront; ~7–8% gross yields (demo figure), mangrove-and-golf lifestyle buyers
comparisons: vs Marjan Bay: quieter, no casino catalyst, but lower entry and protected nature setting

projects.name: DLRC Green Residences
projects.developerTrackRecord: 4 delivered projects, avg. 2 months early handover
projects.roiNotes: Value community, ~7% gross yields; popular with first-time investors for the low ticket
comparisons: vs JVC: cheaper entry, slightly less established but improving connectivity

faqs.q: I'm a first-time buyer — how does off-plan even work?
faqs.a: You pay a deposit now, instalments as we build, and the balance at handover. You own the unit on completion; many first-timers start here for the low entry.

projects.name: Innovation Heights
projects.location: Dubai Science Park, Al Barsha South, Dubai
projects.developerTrackRecord: 4 delivered projects, avg. 2 months early handover
projects.roiNotes: Tenant base of professionals from the science/biotech cluster; ~6–7% yields
comparisons: vs IMPZ/Production City: similar price, Science Park has a more affluent tenant profile

projects.name: Production City Lofts
projects.developerTrackRecord: 4 delivered projects, avg. 2 months early handover
projects.roiNotes: Highest gross yields in the portfolio (~7–8%) on the lowest entry; budget-investor favourite
comparisons: vs DLRC: comparable entry, IMPZ has slightly higher yields but smaller units
